Fibroblast project
1) TROP-2 Characterization 
2) Replication of Fibroblast phenotypes in PDS+IDS Samples

11.01.2025 Nika Mikhailava

TROP-2 characterization

TROP-2 (TACSTD2) - Clinically important tumor antigen

! Trop-2 is a widely expressed epithelial membrane marker that enables highly effective targeted drug delivery through ADCs.
Saticuzuman govitecan is an ADC in clinical trials for various cancers

Tissues / cells with little to no expression
Mesenchymal cells: fibroblasts, endothelial cells, smooth muscle 
Immune cells: T cells, B cells, macrophages, NK cells 
Neural cells: neurons, glia 
Hepatocytes: very low/absent 
Adipocytes: minimal expression

Is TROP-2 expression exclusive to tumor cells?

Fold change= 1.46
LogFC=0.54

Is TROP-2 expression exclusive to tumor cells?

Does TROP-2 expression change after chemotherapy?

Are there clinical associations with TROP-2 bulk expression ?

Is there association of bulk TROP-2 expression with immune infiltration?

TSI fibroblast phenotypes in PDS+IDS Samples

Do the fibroblasts phenotypes replicate in PDS+IDS samples?

More AOIs from the TSI than from the pure stroma which are from pre samples.

PRE+POST NACT (tsi vs stroma)

POST NACT (tsi vs stroma)

Do the fibroblasts phenotypes replicate in PDS+IDS samples?

Are the TSI fibroblasts associated with the immune exhaustion?

Fibroblasts in close proximity to the tumor–stroma interface (TSI), particularly myofibroblastic CAFs (myCAFs), are the main drivers of extracellular matrix (ECM) production, creating a dense, fibrotic barrier.
Does this matrix-rich stroma restricts immune cell infiltration and function near the tumor?
NK cells, B cells, CD8⁺ T cells, CD4⁺ T cells, and dendritic cells are significantly more abundant in distant stromal regions than at the TSI. 
Overall, immune infiltration is higher in distant stroma, indicating an immune-excluded microenvironment at the tumor–stroma interface.

Genes more expressed in the fibroblasts in the TSI than in distant stroma
